youtube-XAkyxZ_hhDo_file1.show: Yariv Mozer: What Does He Really Think About Palestinians and Israel?
youtube-XAkyxZ_hhDo_file1.desc: In June 2012, queer activists briefly disrupted the screening of "The Invisible Men" at Frameline, the San Francisco International LGBT Film Festival. The film screening was sponsored financially by the Israeli Consulate, as part of the "Brand Israel" campaign (learn more about Brand Israel www.pinkwatchingisrael.com), which aims to promote a hip and progressive image of Israel to distract from Israel's violations of Palestinian human rights and international law. The film's director, Yariv Mozer, who was at the screening courtesy of the consulate, was not actually the target of the protest, which was only demanding that Frameline drop its official relationship with the Israeli government. Mozer, however, was very upset about the protest (which did not interrupt the film itself, but the introduction by Frameline Director KC Price) and followed the activists out of the theater to argue about the cultural boycott of Israel. In the discussion that followed, he revealed some very troubling attitudes toward the Palestinians he claims he is helping with his film.

scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.show: Scooby Doo & Scrappy Doo (1980 TV Series)
scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.desc:
Last Uploaded: January 30, 2022
Updated On: January 24, 2023



Significant changes were made to the show in its second season. Fred, Velma, and Daphne were dropped from the series and Shaggy, Scooby traveled all over the world on their own. Stories were also reduced to 7-minute segments and played at intervals with segments of mysteries but encountered geniune supernatural beings with Shaggy and Scooby did their best to escape and Scrappy did his best to splat. The tone of the stories was also changed to a typed of silly comedy, where the bad guys sometimes even talked directly to the camera. This format was used throughout the second and third seasons.

In the series' fourth and final season its format was changed again. Segments were not rotated with an unrelated canine cartoon starring Petey the Puppy as part of The Scooby & Scrappy-Doo/Puppy Hour. Stories reverted to the more traditional tone Scooby fans were used to except that, with a few exceptions, the supernatural element was eliminated from the show althougher. Shaggy, Scooby, and Scrappy worked for the Fearless Detective Agency, owned by Shaggy's uncle Fearless Shaggyfurt, on cases that involved ordinary criminals committing common crimes. Shaggy's uncle was never seen, but when he had a case, he would telephone the agency and give the three their assignment. At other times they took cases directly form visiting clients. How Shaggy and Scooby came to work for the agency may have been the biggest mystery, because they remained as cowardly as ever and were never enthusiastic about getting a case.

An additional segment was included in the fourth season that starred Scrappy-Doo and his (other) Uncle Yabba-Doo. Stories took place in the remote Western town of Tumbleweed, where Scrappy and Yabba assisted the town deputy, Dusty, in maintaing law and order.

As was customary with the Scooby-Doo cartoons, before long the show was reformatted and presented under a different title in order to keep things fresh. In 1983 a new series was produced called The New Scooby & Scrappy-Doo Show. Scrappy-Doo was also later featured in The New Scooby-Doo Mysteries (1984) and The 13 Ghosts of Scooby-Doo (1985). Other shows with a and Shaggy and Scooby-Doo Get a Clue! (2005).



Note 01: The last previous set of Scooby-Doo & Scrappy-Doo (1980 TV Series) had the shorts below 480p video image quality with some very old recordings as well. However, I was able to converge them by using the best quality films found in Czech and speed videos up a pinch to match each audio along with episodes. So I used the old recordings to copy and paste English audios into each video with better quality after muting Czech audios.

Note 02 (Update): 😊 A fellow patreon named Randy Miller has just resolved two videos of its features for Season 04! He was able to converge each segment into the best video quality (for 04-04B - Takeout Stakeout and 04-12 - Up a Crazy River) and then connect them together as one. Hurray!!

I have now inserted those two episdoes in full version, so please do download episodes 04-04 & 04-12 at the fullest version along with Season 04 (...and bypass the old segments as I tried to removed them and they didn't work). Also, full credits goes to Randy Miller (7randymiler@gmail.com) for finding the links with Google Drive and converging the videos into the best qualities for those two episdoes as well.



The email link you can contact me is louieseward@gmail.com. Many thanks, and enjoy!

scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.desc:
Last Uploaded: January 30, 2022
Updated On: January 24, 2023



Significant changes were made to the show in its second season. Fred, Velma, and Daphne were dropped from the series and Shaggy, Scooby traveled all over the world on their own. Stories were also reduced to 7-minute segments and played at intervals with segments of mysteries but encountered geniune supernatural beings with Shaggy and Scooby did their best to escape and Scrappy did his best to splat. The tone of the stories was also changed to a typed of silly comedy, where the bad guys sometimes even talked directly to the camera. This format was used throughout the second and third seasons.

In the series' fourth and final season its format was changed again. Segments were not rotated with an unrelated canine cartoon starring Petey the Puppy as part of The Scooby & Scrappy-Doo/Puppy Hour. Stories reverted to the more traditional tone Scooby fans were used to except that, with a few exceptions, the supernatural element was eliminated from the show althougher. Shaggy, Scooby, and Scrappy worked for the Fearless Detective Agency, owned by Shaggy's uncle Fearless Shaggyfurt, on cases that involved ordinary criminals committing common crimes. Shaggy's uncle was never seen, but when he had a case, he would telephone the agency and give the three their assignment. At other times they took cases directly form visiting clients. How Shaggy and Scooby came to work for the agency may have been the biggest mystery, because they remained as cowardly as ever and were never enthusiastic about getting a case.

An additional segment was included in the fourth season that starred Scrappy-Doo and his (other) Uncle Yabba-Doo. Stories took place in the remote Western town of Tumbleweed, where Scrappy and Yabba assisted the town deputy, Dusty, in maintaing law and order.

As was customary with the Scooby-Doo cartoons, before long the show was reformatted and presented under a different title in order to keep things fresh. In 1983 a new series was produced called The New Scooby & Scrappy-Doo Show. Scrappy-Doo was also later featured in The New Scooby-Doo Mysteries (1984) and The 13 Ghosts of Scooby-Doo (1985). Other shows with a and Shaggy and Scooby-Doo Get a Clue! (2005).

the-oblongs-s-01-e-01-misfit-love_file12.desc:
The Oblongs is an American adult animated sitcom created by Angus Oblong and Jace Richdale. It was Mohawk Productions' first venture into animation. The series premiered on April 1, 2001, on The WB, and was cancelled due to low ratings on May 20, leaving the last five episodes unaired.[1] The remaining episodes were later aired on Cartoon Network's late-night programming block Adult Swim in August 2002, with the series premiering on the network in production order. The series is loosely based on a series of characters introduced in a picture book entitled Creepy Susie and 13 Other Tragic Tales for Troubled Children.[2]

Warner Bros. won the bidding for the rights to turn Angus Oblong's characters into a series; Fox and ABC also made unsuccessful bids. The show was produced by Film Roman, Oblong Productions, Jobsite Productions and Mohawk Productions in association with Warner Bros. Television, and the theme song for the series was composed and performed by They Might Be Giants. This was the only animated series to date to have been produced by Bruce Helford under Mohawk.

A total of 13 episodes were produced. All thirteen episodes of The Oblongs were released on DVD on October 4, 2005.

kihachiro-kawamoto-film-works_file1.desc:
Born in 1925, from an early age Kihachirō Kawamoto was captivated by the art of doll and puppet making. After seeing the works of maestro Czech animator Jiří Trnka, he first became interested in stop motion puppet animation and during the 50s began working alongside Japan's first stop motion animator, the legendary Tadahito Mochinaga. In 1958, he co-founded Shiba Productions to make commercial animation for television, but it was not until 1963, when he traveled to Prague to study puppet animation under Jiří Trnka for a year, that his puppets truly began to take on a life of their own. Trnka encouraged Kawamoto to draw on his own country's rich cultural heritage in his work, and so Kawamoto returned from Czechoslovakia to make a series of highly individual, independently-produced artistic short works, beginning with Breaking of Branches is Forbidden (Hana-Ori) in 1968. Heavily influence by the traditional aesthetics of Noh, Bunraku doll theatre and Kabuki, since the 70s his haunting puppet animations such as The Demon (Oni, 1972), Dōjōji Temple (Dōjōji, 1976) and House of Flame (Kataku, 1979) have won numerous prizes internationally. He has also produced cut-out (kirigami) animations such as The Trip (Tabi, 1973) and A Poet's Life (Shijin no Shogai, 1974). In 1990 he returned to Trnka's studios in Prague to make Briar Rose, or The Sleeping Beauty. A selection of Kihachiro Kawamoto's shorts are currently available on DVD from Geneon: Kihachiro Kawamoto Film Works.

Breaking of Branches is Forbidden (花折り, Hana-Ori?, 1968, 14 min.)
Anthropo-Cynical Farce (Farce anthropo-cynique, 1970, 8 min., from a story by Riichi Yokomitsu)
The Demon (鬼, Oni?, 1972, 8 min.)
Travel (旅, Tabi?, 1973, 12 min.)
A Poet's Life (詩人の生涯, Shijin no Shōgai?, 1974, 19 min., from a story by Kōbō Abe)
Dōjōji (道成寺, 1976, 19 min., from the Nō play of the same name?)
House of Flame (火宅, Kataku?, 1979, 19 min.)
Self-Portrait (セルフポートレート, 1988, 1 min.?)
To Shoot without Shooting (不射之射, Fusha no Sha?, 1988, 25 min.)
Briar-Rose or The Sleeping Beauty (いばら姫またはねむり姫, Ibara-Hime matawa Nemuri-Hime?, 1990, 22 min., from a story by Kyōko Kishida)

HansRuediGiger-Biomechanical-Repository_.desc: "Some people would say my paintings show a future world and maybe they do, but I paint from reality. I put several things and ideas together, and perhaps, when I have finished, it could show the future." - H. R. Giger --------------------- Biography: Hans Ruedi Giger, born on February 5, 1940, in Chur, Switzerland, was a visionary artist renowned for his unique and surreal biomechanical style. Giger's fascination with the macabre and the fantastical can be traced back to his early childhood nightmares, which later fueled his artistic endeavors. He studied architecture and industrial design at the School of Applied Arts in Zurich, where he honed his skills in various mediums. Giger's work is characterized by a fusion of organic and mechanical elements, creating haunting yet mesmerizing landscapes and creatures. Giger gained international acclaim for his work on the film Alien (1979), directed by Ridley Scott. His design of the titular creature earned him an Academy Award for Best Visual Effects. The success of Alien propelled Giger into the spotlight, leading to collaborations with various filmmakers and musicians. His distinctive style also influenced a wide range of media, from video games to album covers, most notably for bands like Emerson, Lake & Palmer and Debbie Harry. Throughout his career, Giger continued to explore themes of birth, death, and the subconscious. His art often evoked a sense of unease, yet it captivated audiences with its intricate detail and originality. Giger passed away on May 12, 2014, in Zurich, leaving behind a legacy that continues to inspire artists and creators worldwide.

DragonBallDaimaAdultSwimBrasil_file38.desc:
Sinopse:
Em Dragon Ball Daima, uma misteriosa conspiração abala o universo: Goku, Vegeta, Bulma e outros heróis são transformados em crianças por forças desconhecidas que parecem ter ligação com o Outro Mundo. Sem a força total que possuíam em suas formas adultas, eles precisam embarcar em uma nova jornada através de mundos inexplorados, enfrentando inimigos perigosos e descobrindo a origem desse estranho feitiço. Agora, com o bastão mágico e a Nuvem Voadora de volta, Goku e seus amigos terão que confiar mais em sua inteligência, coragem e trabalho em equipe do que nunca. Em meio a combates intensos e aventuras cheias de humor e emoção, Daima resgata o espírito clássico de Dragon Ball, enquanto revela uma ameaça que pode ser ainda maior do que qualquer outra que eles já enfrentaram.

Produção:
Dragon Ball Daima é o novo anime da franquia Dragon Ball, criado em comemoração aos 40 anos da obra original de Akira Toriyama. A série está sendo produzida pela Toei Animation, com estreia acontecendo em outubro de 2024. A estreia mundial aconteceu em outubro de 2024, com exibição simultânea em diversos países, incluindo Japão, Estados Unidos e Brasil pela plataforma de streaming 'Crunchyroll'.

No Brasil, Dragon Ball Daima teve sua primeira exibição oficial dublada em fevereiro de 2025, quando os três episódios iniciais foram lançados nos cinemas em formato de filme especial, oferecendo aos fãs uma prévia exclusiva da nova fase da franquia. A recepção foi positiva, especialmente pela qualidade da animação e pela nostalgia evocada pelo retorno de Goku e seus amigos em versões infantis. Posteriormente, a série chegou à mídia tradicional no dia 25 de abril de 2025, sendo transmitida pelo canal Adult Swim, conhecido por exibir animações mais voltadas ao público jovem e adulto e pertencente ao mesmo grupo do Cartoon Network. A dublagem brasileira manteve o elenco clássico, e a estreia na televisão trouxe também uma nova leva de fãs que aguardavam acompanhar semanalmente o desenrolar da história.

mister-t-complete-series-1983_file39.desc:
Mister T is an American animated series that aired on NBC on Saturday morning from 1983 to 1985, featuring the popular actor Mr. T. A total of 30 episodes were produced during all three seasons, with the thirteen episodes for Season 1, eleven for Season 2, and six episodes for the third and final season. The series was produced by Ruby-Spears Enterprises.

Reruns were later seen on the USA Cartoon Express in the late 1980s and early 1990s, and more recently as part of Cartoon Network's Adult Swim late-night programming block and sibling network, Boomerang that airs classic cartoons from the Hanna-Barbera, pre-1991 Ruby-Spears, Turner and classic Warner animated libraries. This is also Phil LaMarr's first role.

The cartoon stars Mr. T as a coach to a gymnastics team (with a specific emphasis on members Jeff, Woody, Robin, and Kim), travelling the world while becoming involved in and solving various mysteries.

At the beginning of each episode, a live-action introduction featuring Mr. T himself is shown to explain what is going on. At the end of each episode, Mr. T narrates a moral lesson for the audience.

rec-20231114120847_file0.desc:
By Mordaunt Hall.
  • March 14, 1927.
THE BELOVED ROGUE, with John Barrymore, Conrad Veidt, Marceline Day, W. Lawson Butt, Henry Victor, Slim Summerville, Mack Swain, Angelo Rossitto. Nigel De Brulier, Lucy Beaumont, Otto Matieson, Jane Winton. Rose Dione, Bertram Grassby and Dick Sutherland, written by Paul Bern, directed by Alan Crosland: special prologue with Allan Prior, tenor. At the Strand Theatre.
Those who wish to feast their eyes upon heroics, amazing escapes and marvelous coincidences with an undercurrent of romance, will be satisfied that John Barrymore's portrait of François Villon is served up in a diverting fashion. Others, who may anticipate a cross between Robert Louis Stevenson's study of the vagabond poet and the romantic figure painted by Justin Huntly M'Carthy in "If I Were King," will be disappointed for this current film release, now at the Mark Strand. It is a fanciful affair which has none of the clever and impressive situations that marked Mr. M'Carthy's masterpiece, in the film version of which William Farnum did extraordinarily good work. It is obvious that in The Beloved Rogue," which comes to the screen from Paul Bern's pen, every effort has been made to dodge Mr. M'Carthy's novel and play. At the same time due advantage has been taken of Villon's alleged daring, his penchant for verse, his fellowship with that coterie of swindling beggars, and also of the character of the superstitious Louis XI.
There are moments when Mr. Barrymore appears to be the real conception of Villon, and then there are times when he is just a handsome adventurer. Alan Crosland, producer of this film, delights in extravagances, exaggerations that are presumed to have a popular appeal. This new François Vilion ducks the darts from the King's henchmen and then picks up a snowball and proves that his aim is truer than that of any archer. He slides down roofs, uses the King's catapult to shoot food over to the hungry multitude and then permits himself to be fired over a roof so that he lands in the boudoir of Charlotte de Vauxcelles.
As the ridiculously painted "fool." Mr. Barrymore reminds one of a comic character in a Sunday colored supplement. It is indeed almost incredulous that this Gaston-like creature is the handsome John Barrymore. But seeing is believing, for Mr. Barrymore with the aid of artificial snow gradually rubs off the coloring, straightens the nose that made him look a bit like Cyrano de Bergerac, cleans off his Mephistophelian eyebrows, wipes away the smudgy whiskers, and then appears as the romantic Mr. Vilion. as created by Mr. Bern. with just a suggestion of Mr. Villon as he might have been.
Here one has none other than Dick Sutherland, whose looks are such that, as the King's executioner, it seems highly probable that his victims might shuffle off through sheer fright before he has had time to employ the axe. The King is brutal with this Tristian l'Hermite, and the executioner takes every insult without flinching, standing perfectly still to receive a glass of wine from the royal hand in his forbidding countenance.
Conrad Veidt, that competent German character actor, fills the rôle of the inquisitive and crafty Louis. Mr. Veidt gives a sound performance, making this whimsical ruler perhaps a little too healthy and well nourished in appearance. It is a part in which Fritz Leiber excelled, and while Mr. Veidt's characterization is satisfactory it does not measure up to Mr. Lieber's brilliant impersonation of the Fox film version of "If I Were King."
A strange dwarf is called upon to lighten some of the episodes, but actually the whole narrative is so flimsy that it needs no such comedy relief. The mere situations in which this Villon finds himself afford amusement, and even when the resourceful hero is imprisoned in a basket affair and dangled outside Charlotte's castle window one has no great compassion for him. He had up to that time outwitted sons of Kings and Dukes, and there really is no reason why he should not be able to free himself at the psychological moment. Would any film producer ever dare to put on a picture in which Mr. Barrymore as the hero perished through either fire or sword? Incidentally, this vagabond Villon is strung up close, very close, to angry flames. It is Louis himself who turns up and permits Villon to pass sentence on the ever-abused Duke of Burgundy.
Mr. Barrymore's performance is just as good as his work in "When a Man Loves" and "Don Juan." Marceline Day is appealing as the dark-eyed heroine.
The New York Times Published: March 14, 1927, Copyright © The New York Times

the-boondocks-uncensored-dvd_file105.desc: The Boondocks is an American adult animated black sitcom, created by Aaron McGruder for Cartoon Network's late-night programming block, Adult Swim. It is based upon his manga-influenced comic strip of the same name. The series premiered on November 6, 2005. Sinopse:
Em Dragon Ball Daima, uma misteriosa conspiração abala o universo: Goku, Vegeta, Bulma e outros heróis são transformados em crianças por forças desconhecidas que parecem ter ligação com o Outro Mundo. Sem a força total que possuíam em suas formas adultas, eles precisam embarcar em uma nova jornada através de mundos inexplorados, enfrentando inimigos perigosos e descobrindo a origem desse estranho feitiço. Agora, com o bastão mágico e a Nuvem Voadora de volta, Goku e seus amigos terão que confiar mais em sua inteligência, coragem e trabalho em equipe do que nunca. Em meio a combates intensos e aventuras cheias de humor e emoção, Daima resgata o espírito clássico de Dragon Ball, enquanto revela uma ameaça que pode ser ainda maior do que qualquer outra que eles já enfrentaram.

Produção:
Dragon Ball Daima é o novo anime da franquia Dragon Ball, criado em comemoração aos 40 anos da obra original de Akira Toriyama. A série está sendo produzida pela Toei Animation, com estreia acontecendo em outubro de 2024. A estreia mundial aconteceu em outubro de 2024, com exibição simultânea em diversos países, incluindo Japão, Estados Unidos e Brasil pela plataforma de streaming 'Crunchyroll'.

No Brasil, Dragon Ball Daima teve sua primeira exibição oficial dublada em fevereiro de 2025, quando os três episódios iniciais foram lançados nos cinemas em formato de filme especial, oferecendo aos fãs uma prévia exclusiva da nova fase da franquia. A recepção foi positiva, especialmente pela qualidade da animação e pela nostalgia evocada pelo retorno de Goku e seus amigos em versões infantis. Posteriormente, a série chegou à mídia tradicional no dia 25 de abril de 2025, sendo transmitida pelo canal Adult Swim, conhecido por exibir animações mais voltadas ao público jovem e adulto e pertencente ao mesmo grupo do Cartoon Network. A dublagem brasileira manteve o elenco clássico, e a estreia na televisão trouxe também uma nova leva de fãs que aguardavam acompanhar semanalmente o desenrolar da história.

10cc-RubberBullets1973_file0.desc:
"Rubber Bullets" is a song by the English band 10cc from their self-titled debut album.

Written by Kevin Godley, Lol Creme, and Graham Gouldman and produced by 10cc, "Rubber Bullets" was the band's first number one single in the UK Singles Chart, spending one week at the top in June 1973.[1] It also reached No. 1 in Ireland and No. 3 in Australia, but it fared relatively poorly in the United States where it peaked at only No. 73. A tongue in cheek homage to the 1957 film Jailhouse Rock with a Beach Boys influence, the song attracted some controversy at the time because of the British Army's use of rubber bullets to quell rioting in Northern Ireland.[2]


Recording and impact[edit]

The song features a double-speed guitar solo, created using a technique also used the same year by Mike Oldfield for his Tubular Bells album. In a BBC Radio Walesinterview,[3] guitarist Eric Stewart explained:

“That's a double track solo on that. It's, it's very, very high, of course, going through a Marshall stack, then I slowed the tape to half speed – seven and a half [inches per second] – and recorded it, you know, going [plays singles picked notes slowly] and when you speed it back up you've got an octave up, but there's a screaming fuzz on the top of it, that's an octave higher than it was recorded. So it's a very unusual sound done in that way, just an experiment. Because 10cc, we love to experiment, we used to love to waste time. And having the beauty of having our own studio, we didn't have a clock in there so we weren't restricted.”

Stewart also recalled:

I was amazed, but pleased that the BBC never banned the track, although they limited its airplay, because they thought it was about the ongoing Northern Ireland conflicts. In fact, it was about an Attica State Prison riot like the ones in the old James Cagney films.

— Eric Stewart[4]

Drummer Kevin Godley, living in Ireland in 2018, also denied the lyrics were about Northern Ireland: "We wrote about it before rubber bullets were a thing in Northern Ireland, but it came out around the same time. But it was a visual story or a sound picture.”[2]


Bassist Graham Gouldman remembered:

Kevin and Lol had the chorus and part of the verse but then got stuck. We all loved the chorus and realized it was a hit in itself, so we wanted to persist with it. I chipped in the line 'we've all got balls and brains, but some's got balls and chains.' One of my finer couplets.

— Graham Gouldman[4]

Although the song was not banned by the BBC at the time of release, much later it was banned for the duration of the Persian Gulf War.[5]

In popular culture[edit]

"Rubber Bullets" was used as the theme song to the pilot episode of American animated TV series Superjail!, which aired in May 2007.[6]

The song can be heard, played on a bar jukebox, in the 1975 Finnish television film Simpauttaja, based on books by Heikki Turunen.

The song was featured on the soundtrack of the 1998 film A Soldier's Daughter Never Cries.[7]

Cover versions[edit]

  • Top of the Pops featured an anonymous cover of the song on their 31st volume.[8]
  • Ronski & Exotic performed a cover of the song on their self-titled album released in 1975[9]
  • Vicky Rosti performed a Finnish version of the song called "Puupaukut" on her 1975 album Vicky.[10]
  • The Vindictives covered the song on their 1994 album Party Time for Assholes.[11]
  • The Men They Couldn't Hang featured a cover of the song on their 2011 album 5 Go Mad on the Other Side.[12]

dan-no-sekai-pain-world-episodio-5_file6.desc:
Pain World - Temporada 1 (2025)

🎬 Sobre a Série:

Pain World é uma série animada independente criada por João Guilherme Brandão Neto em 2025.

Inspirada por animes como Neon Genesis Evangelion, misturada com a estética crua de cartoons amadores da internet, Pain World conta uma história emocional, filosófica e muitas vezes depressiva sobre amadurecimento, perda e esperança.

Apesar da animação simples e do orçamento praticamente inexistente, a série se destaca por seu roteiro emocional, personagens cativantes e um forte clima de "fim do mundo" em cada episódio.

📚 Sinopse:

Dan, um garoto comum, se vê envolvido em uma série de eventos catastróficos que vão muito além da compreensão humana.
Ao lado de seus amigos – incluindo o misterioso Shizuka – Dan precisa enfrentar batalhas internas, dilemas existenciais e até a destruição iminente da realidade como ele conhece.

Entre lágrimas, reflexões e episódios com animação limitada, Pain World mergulha o espectador em um mundo onde o que importa não é a qualidade visual, mas a carga emocional.

📈 Classificação Indicativa:

14 anos
Motivos:

  • Temas psicológicos e existenciais

  • Momentos de violência leve

  • Drama emocional intenso

  • Linguagem ocasionalmente inadequada para menores

mister-t-complete-series-1983_file35.episode: Mr. T - S02E05 (Mystery of the Ancient Ancestor).mp4
mister-t-complete-series-1983_file35.desc:
Mister T is an American animated series that aired on NBC on Saturday morning from 1983 to 1985, featuring the popular actor Mr. T. A total of 30 episodes were produced during all three seasons, with the thirteen episodes for Season 1, eleven for Season 2, and six episodes for the third and final season. The series was produced by Ruby-Spears Enterprises.

Reruns were later seen on the USA Cartoon Express in the late 1980s and early 1990s, and more recently as part of Cartoon Network's Adult Swim late-night programming block and sibling network, Boomerang that airs classic cartoons from the Hanna-Barbera, pre-1991 Ruby-Spears, Turner and classic Warner animated libraries. This is also Phil LaMarr's first role.

The cartoon stars Mr. T as a coach to a gymnastics team (with a specific emphasis on members Jeff, Woody, Robin, and Kim), travelling the world while becoming involved in and solving various mysteries.

At the beginning of each episode, a live-action introduction featuring Mr. T himself is shown to explain what is going on. At the end of each episode, Mr. T narrates a moral lesson for the audience.

deep-dive-on-evangelion_file63.show: Deep Dive on Neon Genesis Evangelion (Korean)
deep-dive-on-evangelion_file63.desc:
This is a compilation of now-deleted videos from a Korean YouTuber who created very deep analyses of Evangelion.

According to the YouTuber’s own account, they first deleted the videos after receiving a warning from Studio Khara. They also said that Khara continued to pressure them and threatened legal action even after the videos were taken down.

Many viewers saw the series as unusually thorough and perspective-shifting, and some fans consider it one of the closest interpretations of Evangelion that reflects Anno’s original worldview.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Go7VXAqhkHg

youtube-04pvt2XRwdk_file0.show: The Spoony — Final Fantasy VIII (Part.5) [RUS sub]
youtube-04pvt2XRwdk_file0.desc:
Видео в хронологическом порядке: http://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLFD6DD2C76E56E552 Видео из группы: http://vk.com/the_spoony_experiment http://spoonyexperiment.com Знакомьтесь - Noah Antwiler, называющий себя "ужасным результатом поколения, взращеного на MTV и фильмах Ван Дамма и Стивена Сигала на пике их популярности". Он начал с текстовых обзоров фильмов, которые некоторое время публиковались как "A Gamer's Rant on the Movies" в журнале «Knights Of The Dinner Table». Вскоре он начал делать видео обзоры о играх, и таким образом создал новую личность - The SpoonyOne , (имя заимствовано из определенной цитаты в Final Fantasy IV). Он начал с «The Adventures of Bayou Billy», одной из самых сложных игр на NES," что, вероятно, способствовало тому, чтобы сделать меня таким психически неуравновешенным, каков я есть сегодня"(с), и продолжил с такими играми, как «Zombie Nation», «Heroes of the Lance», и одной и единственной в своём роде "E.T" для Atari 2600. Этот обзор он сделал когда восстанавливался от того,что ему удалили все зубы мудрости."это наверное какой-то мировой рекорд по боли"(с). Он фанат «Babylon Five», «Firefly», «Kolchak: The Night Stalker»,»The X Files» и «Sliders», и жил в доме своих родителей на протяжении более 26 лет, но даже у нёрдов есть свои стандарты, что очевидно в его обзоре на Final Fantasy VIII, где он упоминает Final Fantasy VII и плохо отзывается об отаку или по крайней мере о тех, которые "вынуждены одеваться, как идиоты, и думают, что Neon Genesis Evangelion - это действительно глубокая и серьёзная вещь."(с) С тех пор он перешёл на видео-обзоры фильмов и телевизионных шоу, в том числе и полностью импровизированные отзывы о любом новом релизе который ему не понравится сразу же после того как он его посмотрит (например «Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull» и «Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen» действительно что-то, что стоит увидеть), а также аналогично импровизированные комментарии о реслинге (в настоящее время находящиеся на его отдельном сайте который называется «Wrestle! Wrestle!»), которые часто бывают такой же продолжительности, как и программа, которую он критикует. Кроме того, он сделал полный летс-плей на «Phantasmagoria 2: A Puzzle of Flesh» и прохождение Ripper (в которую он играл в темные времена, когда у игр не было FAQ) и SWAT 4. Так же он стал обзорщиком на «That Guy With The Glasses»

Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=04pvt2XRwdk

DragonBallDaimaAdultSwimBrasil_file17.desc:
Sinopse:
Em Dragon Ball Daima, uma misteriosa conspiração abala o universo: Goku, Vegeta, Bulma e outros heróis são transformados em crianças por forças desconhecidas que parecem ter ligação com o Outro Mundo. Sem a força total que possuíam em suas formas adultas, eles precisam embarcar em uma nova jornada através de mundos inexplorados, enfrentando inimigos perigosos e descobrindo a origem desse estranho feitiço. Agora, com o bastão mágico e a Nuvem Voadora de volta, Goku e seus amigos terão que confiar mais em sua inteligência, coragem e trabalho em equipe do que nunca. Em meio a combates intensos e aventuras cheias de humor e emoção, Daima resgata o espírito clássico de Dragon Ball, enquanto revela uma ameaça que pode ser ainda maior do que qualquer outra que eles já enfrentaram.

Produção:
Dragon Ball Daima é o novo anime da franquia Dragon Ball, criado em comemoração aos 40 anos da obra original de Akira Toriyama. A série está sendo produzida pela Toei Animation, com estreia acontecendo em outubro de 2024. A estreia mundial aconteceu em outubro de 2024, com exibição simultânea em diversos países, incluindo Japão, Estados Unidos e Brasil pela plataforma de streaming 'Crunchyroll'.

No Brasil, Dragon Ball Daima teve sua primeira exibição oficial dublada em fevereiro de 2025, quando os três episódios iniciais foram lançados nos cinemas em formato de filme especial, oferecendo aos fãs uma prévia exclusiva da nova fase da franquia. A recepção foi positiva, especialmente pela qualidade da animação e pela nostalgia evocada pelo retorno de Goku e seus amigos em versões infantis. Posteriormente, a série chegou à mídia tradicional no dia 25 de abril de 2025, sendo transmitida pelo canal Adult Swim, conhecido por exibir animações mais voltadas ao público jovem e adulto e pertencente ao mesmo grupo do Cartoon Network. A dublagem brasileira manteve o elenco clássico, e a estreia na televisão trouxe também uma nova leva de fãs que aguardavam acompanhar semanalmente o desenrolar da história.

raton_esponja_latino_file11.desc:
Ratón esponja es una serie animada estadounidense, emitida inicialmente entre el 19 de julio de 2005 y el 18 de diciembre de 2006 en el bloque de programación Adult Swim de Cartoon Network. El programa fue creado y escrito por Matt Maiellaro y producido por Williams Street. El show se caracteriza por su cruda animación y sus bocetos simplificados, así como por su historia que mezcla suspenso, humor absurdo y thriller psicológico. En Latinoamérica, la serie fue transmitida por Cartoon Network desde el 2 de febrero de 2007 hasta el 7 de marzo de 2008, luego retransmitida por I.Sat desde 2008 hasta 2010 doblada al español, y desde 2015 hasta abril de 2020 subtitulada.

El show se centra en Ratón Fitzgerald o "Fitz", un ratón antropomórfico en busca de respuestas sobre su mundo y su pasado. La historia trata sobre una extraña conspiración, que se construye sobre sí misma, y que en ocasiones llega a ser torcida o no lineal, dejando pistas en cada episodio (incluso en los créditos), pero se va armando hasta develar una historia concreta y profunda.

Tom Goes to the Mayor is an American adult animated sitcom created by Tim Heidecker and Eric Wareheim for Cartoon Network's late night programming block, Adult Swim. It premiered on November 14, 2004 and ended on September 25, 2006 with a total of 30 episodes.

Tom Goes to the Mayor began as a web cartoon on timanderic.com. It was popular enough to get a web sequel, in which David Cross guest stars. Bob Odenkirk noticed the show's potential, and began producing it for Adult Swim.

Pain World é uma série animada independente criada por João Guilherme Brandão Neto em 2025.

Inspirada por animes como Neon Genesis Evangelion, misturada com a estética crua de cartoons amadores da internet, Pain World conta uma história emocional, filosófica e muitas vezes depressiva sobre amadurecimento, perda e esperança.

Apesar da animação simples e do orçamento praticamente inexistente, a série se destaca por seu roteiro emocional, personagens cativantes e um forte clima de "fim do mundo" em cada episódio.

Dan, um garoto comum, se vê envolvido em uma série de eventos catastróficos que vão muito além da compreensão humana.
Ao lado de seus amigos – incluindo o misterioso Shizuka – Dan precisa enfrentar batalhas internas, dilemas existenciais e até a destruição iminente da realidade como ele conhece.

Entre lágrimas, reflexões e episódios com animação limitada, Pain World mergulha o espectador em um mundo onde o que importa não é a qualidade visual, mas a carga emocional.

DragonBallDaimaAdultSwimBrasil_file39.desc:
Sinopse:
Em Dragon Ball Daima, uma misteriosa conspiração abala o universo: Goku, Vegeta, Bulma e outros heróis são transformados em crianças por forças desconhecidas que parecem ter ligação com o Outro Mundo. Sem a força total que possuíam em suas formas adultas, eles precisam embarcar em uma nova jornada através de mundos inexplorados, enfrentando inimigos perigosos e descobrindo a origem desse estranho feitiço. Agora, com o bastão mágico e a Nuvem Voadora de volta, Goku e seus amigos terão que confiar mais em sua inteligência, coragem e trabalho em equipe do que nunca. Em meio a combates intensos e aventuras cheias de humor e emoção, Daima resgata o espírito clássico de Dragon Ball, enquanto revela uma ameaça que pode ser ainda maior do que qualquer outra que eles já enfrentaram.

Produção:
Dragon Ball Daima é o novo anime da franquia Dragon Ball, criado em comemoração aos 40 anos da obra original de Akira Toriyama. A série está sendo produzida pela Toei Animation, com estreia acontecendo em outubro de 2024. A estreia mundial aconteceu em outubro de 2024, com exibição simultânea em diversos países, incluindo Japão, Estados Unidos e Brasil pela plataforma de streaming 'Crunchyroll'.

No Brasil, Dragon Ball Daima teve sua primeira exibição oficial dublada em fevereiro de 2025, quando os três episódios iniciais foram lançados nos cinemas em formato de filme especial, oferecendo aos fãs uma prévia exclusiva da nova fase da franquia. A recepção foi positiva, especialmente pela qualidade da animação e pela nostalgia evocada pelo retorno de Goku e seus amigos em versões infantis. Posteriormente, a série chegou à mídia tradicional no dia 25 de abril de 2025, sendo transmitida pelo canal Adult Swim, conhecido por exibir animações mais voltadas ao público jovem e adulto e pertencente ao mesmo grupo do Cartoon Network. A dublagem brasileira manteve o elenco clássico, e a estreia na televisão trouxe também uma nova leva de fãs que aguardavam acompanhar semanalmente o desenrolar da história.

the-boondocks-uncensored-dvd_file103.desc: The Boondocks is an American adult animated black sitcom, created by Aaron McGruder for Cartoon Network's late-night programming block, Adult Swim. It is based upon his manga-influenced comic strip of the same name. The series premiered on November 6, 2005. The show focuses on a Black American family, the Freemans, settling into the fictional, friendly and predominantly White suburb of Woodcrest. The perspective offered by this mixture of cultures, lifestyles, social classes, stereotypes, viewpoints and racialized identities provides for much of the series' satire, comedy, and conflict.
